subject: [PATCH v5 2/7] NFSD: Add NFSD_CMD_UNLOCK_IP netlink command

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that svc_addr_len() is called even when tracing is disabled because it's part of the argument list and thus not skipped by the static branch in trace_nfsd_ctl_unlock_ip(), and suggested determining the length inside the tracepoint to avoid unnecessary calculation
reviews.raw_body:
Unless I'm mistaken, the trace_nfsd_ctl_unlock_ip() call site
expands to a static branch that skips everything, including
argument evaluation, when the tracepoint is disabled. The
svc_addr_len() call, being part of the argument list, is
already behind that branch.

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the tracepoint macro TP_STRUCT__entry() requires the length value upfront to properly size the ring buffer entry, and suggested passing the length directly instead of calculating it later.
reviews.raw_body:
The TP_STRUCT__entry( ) macro needs the length value up front
to size the ring buffer entry. This is the cleanest way to do it.

2026-03-27.patch_summary: This patch modifies NFSD tracepoints to determine the length of svc_addr_len(sap) directly, eliminating the need for a separate calculation when tracing is enabled.
